level_signals: 6+ years of direct product management experience
Comp range $191K-$225K base (Zone 1), consistent with Staff IC
At least 2 years shipping zero-to-one products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years as PM of direct product management experience building high-quality consumer products
at least 2 years of experience shipping zero to one products
Demonstrated history of using research, experimentation and analytics to guide decision making
Experience working with and coordinating across globally dispersed teams
Past experience working with engineering, design, research, legal, compliance and marketing in previous roles
Passion for financial products that improve people's financial lives.
Willingness to work with a global team and EST hours

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Ideally past experience working on a global consumer facing product

scope.surface: International product expansion — UK, 31 EU/EEA countries, and new markets globally
core_responsibilities: Define strategy for new country launches and how to win new markets for Robinhood.
Define roadmaps as well as collaborate with teams to execute them in a timely manner.
Work with global teams to ship groundbreaking products into new regions with unique customer needs and operational requirements.
Bring together product development teams across the company with a clear product vision and shared set of priorities.
Develop strong, collaborative relationships across design, engineering, and operations at the team and organizational levels.
Ensure parity of product lines across global markets balancing localized product innovation and new US products.

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total rewards (stock options/RSUs implied)

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($191K-$225K base for Zone 1)
Equity and bonus mentioned in addition to base pay
Clear product surface and ownership area (international expansion, specific markets named)
Comprehensive benefits package including 100% paid health insurance, family forming benefits, mental health support
Strong mission-driven culture with specific international market context (UK + 31 EU/EEA countries)
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 3 days per week in office; Menlo Park or New York only — eliminates remote candidates
Requirement to work EST hours even when based in Menlo Park, CA may be burdensome for West Coast employees
'Ideally' qualifier on global consumer product experience is soft but likely strongly preferred for a staff-level international role
No explicit mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for international candidates applying to a global role
Significant travel expectation with no further specification of frequency or regions

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Staff' title and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not in JD)
compensation.equity_form (inferred; JD only says 'equity' without specifying form)
ambiguities: Base pay range shown for Zone 1 (Menlo Park, NY) as $191K-$225K; role location is Menlo Park or New York — both are Zone 1, so range is role-relevant, but Zone 2/3 ranges are also listed for other locations. Used Zone 1 as primary since those are the listed role locations.
The requirement to work 'EST hours' while the role is based in Menlo Park, CA (PT) could create scheduling burden — the JD does not clarify whether this is mandatory or aspirational.

Several items in 'What you bring' use unconditional bullet phrasing but 'Ideally' is used for global consumer product experience — all non-'Ideally' bullets treated as hard requirements per schema rules.
